name: feat-ESE-0012-04
title: Buffer sync: upload, dispatch, readback lifecycle
description:
Implement the buffer sync system: uploading CPU TypedArrays to GPU before dispatch, dispatching compute passes, and reading back GPU-written buffers to CPU TypedArrays after dispatch. Integrates with the existing pipeline phases via sync points.

Physics authority model: CPU game logic writes intent components (GpuForce, GpuImpulse, GpuTeleport) in preUpdate. Upload syncs these + current Transform/Velocity to GPU. GPU physics runs in update. Readback copies GPU-written Transform/Velocity back before postUpdate. GPU-authoritative components (GpuParticleLife, GpuParticleVisual) skip readback.

Inspired by Bevy/Rapier's SyncBackendâ†’StepSimulationâ†’Writeback pipeline and bevy_hanabi's zero-sync-for-GPU-only data. See engine/gpu/architecture.md sections 3 and 7.
acceptanceCriteria:
- uploadBuffers(): copies dirty CPU TypedArrays to corresponding GPUBuffers via queue.writeBuffer
- Upload includes intent components (GpuForce, GpuImpulse, GpuTeleport) written by CPU game logic
- dispatchKernel(): creates command encoder, compute pass, sets pipeline + bind group, dispatches workgroups, submits
- readbackBuffers(): maps GPU-written buffers back to CPU TypedArrays for components in the kernel write set
- createGpuSystem(): wraps upload-dispatch-readback into a SystemFn compatible with addSystem(). Registers write claims on GpuContext for authority guards.
- GPU-authoritative components (marked as such) skip readback unless explicitly requested
- Intent components (GpuForce, GpuImpulse) zeroed by GPU kernel after consumption â€” readback reflects zeroed state
- Unit tests for upload correctness, dispatch execution, readback correctness, intent consumption
demoDeliverable: Create a world with 100 entities having Transform + Velocity + GpuRigidBody. In preUpdate, a CPU system applies GpuForce to 10 entities and GpuTeleport to 1 entity. GPU physics kernel reads intents, integrates, writes Transform + Velocity. After readback, show: (1) teleported entity at new position, (2) forced entities accelerated, (3) GpuForce/GpuImpulse zeroed after consumption. Also show authority guard: CPU attempt to write Transform directly on a GpuRigidBody entity throws.
testingScenarios:
- Upload: CPU array [1,2,3] to GPU buffer, readback matches [1,2,3]
- Dispatch: GPU kernel that doubles values, readback shows doubled values
- Dirty tracking: unchanged components are not re-uploaded
- Intent flow: CPU writes GpuForce(10,0,0) on entity, GPU reads it, integrates velocity, readback shows updated velocity and zeroed force
- Teleport flow: CPU writes GpuTeleport(50,50,50,active=1), GPU applies it, readback shows position (50,50,50) and active=0
- Capacity growth: adding entities beyond initial capacity triggers buffer regrow + re-upload
- Concurrent access: CPU system reads Transform after GPU system writes it (readback completes before CPU phase)
- GPU-authoritative skip: particle component marked GPU-only is not read back each frame
- Authority guard integration: createGpuSystem registers write claims, CPU writes blocked on guarded entities
stakeholderUnderstanding: Buffer sync is the glue between CPU and GPU worlds. It handles the uploadâ†’dispatchâ†’readback lifecycle and wraps it into a standard SystemFn that plugs into our existing pipeline phases. The ownership model (GPU writes in update, CPU reads after readback in postUpdate) is what makes shared components like Transform safe. GPU-authoritative components skip readback for performance.
